channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: A presidential first: Trump at the Super Bowl, latest chapter in a complicated legacy with football
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — As a student, Donald Trump played high school football. As a business baron, he owned a team in an upstart rival to the NFL and then sued the established league.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: They helped US order airstrikes against Taliban. Now Trump's moves have left those Afghans in limbo
item.description: TIRANA, Albania (AP) — They helped the U.S. military order airstrikes against Taliban and Islamic State fighters and worked as drivers and translators during America's longest war. They were set to start new lives in the United States.
item.dc:creator: LLAZAR SEMINI, FARNOUSH AMIRI and MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Kosovo votes for new parliament as foreign aid dwindles and talks with Serbia are stalled
item.description: PRISTINA, Kosovo (AP) — Kosovars cast their votes Sunday in a parliamentary election considered a key test for Prime Minister Albin Kurti as talks on normalizing ties with rival Serbia remain stalled and foreign funding for one of Europe's poorest countries in question.
item.dc:creator: LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 2 suspects, including victim's husband, charged in stabbing death of Fort Campbell soldier in Tennessee
item.description: Two people were charged in last year's stabbing death of a Fort Campbell soldier in Tennessee, police said, noting that one of the suspects was the victim's husband.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Unusual video from Florida police shows note on 'first date' brick woman allegedly hurled through ex's window
item.description: Local authorities in Florida release an unusual video of a woman throwing a brick through her ex-boyfriend's window after she initially denied the act and was arrested.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What makes a dog a winner? A Westminster Kennel Club judge explains
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The competitors can come in forms as different as a Chihuahua, a great Dane, a bulldog and a greyhound. Each one is a champion.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House Republicans mull taxes on scholarships and changes to student loan programs
item.description: As Republicans in Congress look for ways to slash spending, some legislators are floating new taxes on college scholarships, an end to student loan repayment plans and a big hike in taxes on university endowments.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne Mumphrey, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: In Iowa's farm country, small towns rally around a native son in football's biggest game
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — In the farm town where Cooper DeJean drew up football plays in elementary school and taught classmates to run them at recess, residents plan a huge party to watch the town’s native son in the Super Bowl.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Hawaii is the rainbow capital of the world. Here's what that means
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — Hawaii's regular sunshine, short rain showers and clean air come together to create some of the planet's best conditions for viewing rainbows. They're such a frequent sight that a University of Hawaii professor calls the state the “rainbow capital of the world.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Here's what we know about a commuter plane crash in Alaska that killed 10 people
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — Authorities are working to recover the wreckage of a plane crash in western Alaska that killed 10 people while investigators are trying to determine what caused the small commuter aircraft to go down in the icy Bering Sea.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Anthony Davis leaves his Dallas debut with an injury after seismic Luka Doncic trade
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — Anthony Davis left in the third quarter with a lower-body injury and didn't return in his debut with the Dallas Mavericks on Saturday after the seismic trade that sent Luka Doncic to the Los Angeles Lakers.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Recall for broccoli sold at Walmart in 20 states upgraded to highest risk level: FDA
item.description: The Food and Drug Administration has upgraded its classification of a broccoli recall initially reported in late December, labeling it as “Class I” — the agency’s most serious recall category.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: Missing Alaska flight found with no survivors: What to know
item.description: The plane, operated by Bering Air, had been traveling from Unalakleet to Nome when it vanished on Thursday, only to be found the next day.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, KTLA 5

item.title: Nelly delivers hits at 'Homecoming' Super Bowl week concert in historic New Orleans restaurant
item.description: Nelly brought his signature St. Louis swagger to a New Orleans institution, turning a nearly seven-decade-old restaurant into a Super Bowl week party.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Landrum Jr, WFLA, NBC

item.title: PlayStation Network back up after dealing with technical issues since Friday
item.description: (KRON) -- The PlayStation Network announced late Saturday afternoon that "all services are up and running" after it experienced issues across its platforms for almost 24 hours. Users were first alerted of service issues at 4 p.m. on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Tor Smith, KRON4

item.title: Ohio city files federal lawsuit against alleged neo-Nazi group
item.description: The city of Springfield, Ohio has filed a lawsuit against an alleged neo-Nazi group for conducting what it claims to be a campaign of threats and intimidation.
item.dc:creator: LeAnne Marie McPherson, NewsNation

item.title: Tips to easily cancel your subscriptions
item.description: The FTC says that it received about 70 consumer complaints a day in 2024 about recurring subscriptions, up from 42 per day in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Kimberly Palmer, NewsNation

item.title: LeBron James will sit out the Lakers' game against Indiana to rest his sore left ankle
item.description: LeBron James is sitting out the Los Angeles Lakers ' game against Indiana on Saturday to rest his sore left ankle.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: A tax credit worth up to $7,830 often goes unclaimed: See who qualifies for the EITC
item.description: The IRS estimates that a fifth of eligible taxpayers fail to take advantage of the tax break each year.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Woman is accused of stealing from a TV reporter found dead at his hotel before the Super Bowl
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Police are investigating the death of a television reporter who was in New Orleans to cover the Super Bowl and on Friday announced the arrest of a woman they say used his credit card.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: T-Swift fan, Philly agency trolling each other ahead of Super Bowl LIX
item.description: A Taylor Swift fan from Boston and a Philadelphia-based advertising agency have spent the week leading up to Super Bowl LIX trolling each other with billboards in KC and Philly.
item.dc:creator: Dillon Seckington, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Latino group calls for boycott of Coca-Cola, Walmart and more over deportations
item.description: This is part of an effort by a group called Freeze Latino Movement to boycott American companies while poking fun of the ongoing deportations launched by the Trump Administration and Immigration and Customs Enforcement, ICE.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump signs order to establish White House Faith Office
item.description: Trump directed the office to identify ways to reduce burdens on the free exercise of religion and direct all federal agencies to designate a “faith liaison” within 90 days.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: For high school sports, decisions loom: Follow Trump or state law on transgender athletes
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — Minnesota's governing body for high school sports says it will follow state law — not President Donald Trump's executive order — and continue to allow transgender athletes to compete in prep athletics.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Alaska Legislature asks Trump to retain Denali's name instead of change it to Mount McKinley
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — The Alaska Legislature passed a resolution Friday urging President Donald Trump to reverse course and retain the name of North America’s tallest peak as Denali rather than change it to Mount McKinley.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judges curb Trump’s sweeping executive actions
item.description: Federal judges are curbing President Trump’s sweeping directives to reshape the government, issuing a flurry of rulings blocking his agenda from charging forward.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Musk hails Trump's vow to bring back plastic straws
item.description: (KRON) -- President Donald Trump took to Truth Social Friday to slam paper straws and promise to go "BACK TO PLASTIC," in his preferred all-caps method of communicating on social media.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Live: Breaking down the matchups headed into Super Bowl weekend
item.description: The Chiefs and Eagles will kickoff Super Bowl LIX in two days from the Caesars Superdome in New Orleans, both with hopes of being crowned the kings of the NFL.
item.dc:creator: Juan Cisneros, NewsNation

item.title: Dog Show 101: What's what at the Westminster Kennel Club
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Westminster Kennel Club dog show is back for a 149th year — and back at New York's Madison Square Garden for the first time since early 2020.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Sports reporter dies while in New Orleans for Super Bowl LIX
item.description: Adan Manzano died on Wednesday in New Orleans while on assignment to cover the Super Bowl for both Telemundo Kansas City and Tico Sports.
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Mueller, ABC 4

item.title: 500,000 LG electric ovens recalled after fires, pet deaths
item.description: There are 86 reports of unintentional activation of the front knobs, 28 fires, at least eight injuries, and three pet deaths, per the Consumer Product Safety Commission.
item.dc:creator: Brady Doran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Second type of bird flu found in US dairy cows
item.description: Experts said it raises new questions about wider spread and the difficulty of controlling infections in animals and the people who work closely with them.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Crayola brings back 8 retired colors: Here's what they are
item.description: Crayola is going back to the drawing board with its latest collection, bringing back a special assortment of retired colors for the first time in its 122-year history.
item.dc:creator: Brady Doran, Fox 5

item.title: New NASA imagery shows large asteroid that could impact Earth in 2032
item.description: A recently released animation shows a new look at a large asteroid that has a small, but significant, chance of impacting planet Earth in seven years.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, Fox 5

item.title: Japan Airlines plane clips Delta flight at Sea-Tac airport
item.description: A plane at Seattle-Tacoma International Airport hit another aircraft that was taxiing on the runway Wednesday morning, the Federal Aviation Administration has confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: As case involving Prince Harry's immigration paperwork inches forward, judge considers next steps
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge is considering next steps in a slow-moving court case over whether to release documents that could spell legal trouble for Prince Harry, with an influential conservative think tank seeking to reveal if he lied on his immigration paperwork about past drug use or rece
item.dc:creator: ASHRAF KHALIL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Storm threatens significant ice accumulations in several mid-Atlantic states, forecasters say
item.description: CHARLESTON, W.Va. (AP) — A winter storm approaching several mid-Atlantic states is threatening significant ice accumulations that could cause widespread power outages and disrupt travel, forecasters warned Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4
